untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of double bottom pattern formation (two distinct bottoms at similar price levels) and throwback/pullback behavior, which cannot be expressed using point-in-time technical indicators. Pattern recognition requires multi-bar lookback logic and peak/valley detection not available in the allowed indicator set. Pivot point used as proxy for 'confirmation point' but this is an imperfect substitute.
translation_notes: This signal fundamentally describes a candlestick pattern requiring historical shape analysis. The entry conditions demand: (1) identifying two bottoms (A and B), (2) finding the highest high between them as confirmation point, (3) detecting a breakout above that level, (4) confirming a throwback, then (5) buying on recovery. None of these can be reliably detected without multi-bar pattern recognition logic. The translated rule uses close > pivot as a weak proxy for recovery above confirmation, but this does not capture the double-bottom geometry or throwback requirement. A proper implementation requires custom pattern detection code outside standard indicators.
